description: Annualized demand CAGR from base year to 2035 (policy target horizon)
formula: per material: ((D_2035/D_base)^(1/n) - 1)*100; flattened by mean across materials
citation_or_rationale: CAGR convention; IEA WEO 2024; Graedel et al. 2015

description: Annualized demand CAGR from base year to 2050 (full horizon)
formula: per material: ((D_2050/D_base)^(1/n) - 1)*100; flattened by mean across materials

description: Maximum YoY % demand change pre-2035 averaged across materials
formula: per material: max((D_t+1 - D_t)/D_t * 100) for years <= 2035; flattened by mean
citation_or_rationale: PJ (pre-PI): captures spike vs gradual buildout (the qualitative scenario distinction)

description: Mean coefficient of variation of demand across MC iterations
formula: mean across materials and years of (std/mean) of MC samples
citation_or_rationale: MC convention; Graedel et al. 2012 supply-risk uncertainty framing

description: Solar (PV+CSP) capacity share at 2035
Wind capacity share at 2035
Solar (PV+CSP) capacity share at 2050
Wind capacity share at 2050
Material-level demand CAGR from base year to 2050
Coefficient of variation of total demand across the 61 scenarios
Net Import Reliance (NIR) per USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries methodology
formula: (imports - exports)/consumption per year averaged; clipped to [0,1]

description: Governance-weighted import concentration HHI per EU CRM methodology
citation_or_rationale: Blengini et al. 2017 (EU CRM); Schrijvers et al. 2020

description: Global production HHI per Graedel criticality framework
citation_or_rationale: Graedel et al. 2012 ES&T; EU CRM

description: HHI of US import sources
Mean US demand relative to US domestic production
Mean US demand relative to global production (single-country squeeze)
citation_or_rationale: Demand-vs-production framework (PI 2026-04-08: need both US and global)

description: How many years of US transition demand the global reserve base supports
citation_or_rationale: USGS reserve reporting; Graedel et al. 2012 static reserve framework

description: US share of global reserves (geographic self-sufficiency; demand-independent)
citation_or_rationale: Replaces domestic_reserve_coverage which had r=1.000 with global_reserve_coverage (both shared cumulative_demand denominator)

description: China's share of global reserves
Fraction of global reserves in high-risk jurisdictions (CRC>=5 + China)
formula: sum(reserves where CRC>=5 or China) / global_reserves

description: Direct US dependence on China as a single supplier
